page: Amazon Web Services c8g.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. Built on the arm64 architecture, this server features 8 dedicated physical cores with a single thread per core, operating at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz. The CPU configuration includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. The instance runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, lacks local storage, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps. No GPU accelerators are present.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1883.0 and a stress-ng single-core CPU score of 1706.99. Memory bandwidth for large, potentially uncached blocks is strong at 188.91 GB/sec, while cached small-block read bandwidth reaches 924.79 GB/sec. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks perform well, with gzip compression at 30.34 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 14.22 GB/sec. However, cryptographic performance is a notable bottleneck, as evidenced by a weak Passmark CPU encryption score of 1509.87.

This instance is designed for compute-bound workloads that benefit from dedicated physical cores and high memory bandwidth, such as web serving, text processing, and software compilation. It achieves a static web serving throughput of 19.82 GB/sec and a Redis SET operations rate of 18.14 million operations per second. While it handles small LLM inference tasks adequately, prompt processing for medium 7B models is poor at 15.58 tokens/sec. The arm64 architecture offers qualitative cost efficiency by delivering competitive compute density and high memory bandwidth per core compared to traditional x86 architectures, though it lacks specialized hardware acceleration for cryptography or machine learning.
description: Amazon Web Services c8g.2xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized instance powered by an 8-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 16.0 GB of memory, yielding 2.0 GB per core, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor with a 4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth and no local storage. Benchmarks show strong single-core capabilities, including a Geekbench single-core score of 1883.0 and strong uncached memory read bandwidth of 188.91 GB/sec. However, cryptographic performance is weak, with a Passmark encryption score of 1509.87. The instance is suited for compute-intensive tasks such as text processing, software compilation, and web serving, where its dedicated physical cores provide consistent execution. It offers a resource-efficient profile for arm64-native workloads but is limited for heavy cryptographic or large-scale machine learning tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8g.2xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized instance with 8 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 16.0 GB of memory, and strong single-core and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8g.2xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized instance featuring 8 Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 16.0 GB RAM, and a Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: An arm64 compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated physical cores and strong memory bandwidth for text processing, web serving, and compilation workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an 8-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 arm64 processor at 2.8 GHz
Equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory and 36 MB of L3 cache
Delivers strong single-core performance and uncached memory read bandwidth
Operates on the Nitro hypervisor with 4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Suited for web serving, text processing, and software compilation
Exhibits lower performance in cryptographic and medium-model LLM tasks
